pattern: Sort points by x ascending then y descending so that for each Alice candidate all valid Bob candidates appear as a suffix, then a sweep over decreasing y finds enclosed third-party points efficiently.
hints: Alice must be strictly up-left of Bob (Alice.x <= Bob.x, Alice.y >= Bob.y); both equalities are allowed because the rectangle can be a line.

After sorting by x ascending, y descending, for each Alice at index i scan all j>i where points[j].x >= points[i].x and points[j].y <= points[i].y, but reject pairs where any other point lies strictly inside or on the rectangle boundary.
Track the maximum y seen so far among Bob candidates to quickly discard enclosed points: a third point between Alice and Bob in x must not have y in [Bob.y, Alice.y].

edge_inputs.description: three points on anti-diagonal, two valid pairs
only two points, they form one valid pair
two points at same x, vertical fence is valid
third point exactly on fence boundary is disqualifying
four corners of a rectangle, central pair blocks outer pairs

stress.description: random small distinct points, brute force is tractable
